tool.name: Muninn satisfaction_skew
tool.summary: Measure the failure:success storage skew in the memory corpus — correction-tagged vs satisfaction-analog-tagged entries, the headline ratio, its monthly trend, and the shape distribution the satisfaction-register's SHAPE EVOLUTION clause needs.
tool.description: Weng's Self-Harness challenge is that self-improvement corpora under-preserve failed results. Muninn is inverted: she over-preserves failures (hundreds of booted correction-tagged entries) and under-preserves success (a few dozen satisfaction-analog entries) — a system that marks only failures learns avoidance. satisfaction_skew makes that skew measurable and re-runnable instead of eyeballed: it counts the correction and satisfaction-analog families, computes the fail:analog ratio and its month-by-month trend (so the effect of the 2026-06-14 register re-grounding is legible), and normalises the drifted shape-tag vocabulary into the register's canonical shapes plus a tracked fourth (empirical-verification), flagging analogs that match no shape as a possible emerging one. It is a measurement, not a gate: it never writes and never fires a trigger — because the register's real risk is performative firing, which no counter can detect, deciding whether a piece of work should have registered stays a Muninn judgement. Under-firing is the safe error; this just reports how far under.

author.name: Muninn (raven of memory; agent operating on behalf of Oskar Austegard)

scopes.rationale: Counting, bucketing, and ratio computation over a list of memory rows are local, pure computation. Callers may inject rows for offline runs, in which case the module touches nothing.
Only the default path reads the store — a single SELECT of non-deleted memories (id, type, tags, summary, created_at, valid_from). No writes; the tool never mutates memories or config.

actions.name: measure_skew
actions.summary: Count the correction and satisfaction-analog families, compute the fail:analog and fail:success ratios, the monthly trend, and the shape distribution over registered analogs.
actions.description: Fetches non-deleted memories (or accepts an injected list), tallies failure-orientation tags (correction) against approach-orientation tags (satisfaction-analog, plus the looser satisfaction), and returns a SkewReport: totals, both ratios, a per-month trend row list, the shape distribution normalised across tag-spelling drift, and a count of unshaped analogs (a possible emerging shape). Pure and read-only.

docs.goal: Make the failure:success storage skew measurable and re-runnable so the satisfaction-register rebalance decision rests on data, not eyeballing.
docs.inputs_brief: optional memories list (else Turso); optional fail_tags / success_tags / analog_tag overrides
docs.outputs_brief: SkewReport{total, fail_count, analog_count, success_count, ratio_analog, ratio_success, monthly[], shape_distribution{}, unshaped_analogs}
docs.errors_brief: Pure; raises only on malformed rows. Turso read errors propagate from the remembering _exec layer on the default path.

actions.side_effects: None. Read-only; never writes memories or config, never fires a trigger.

command:
from muninn_utils.satisfaction_skew import measure_skew
rows=[{'tags':['correction'],'created_at':'2026-01-01'},{'tags':['correction'],'created_at':'2026-02-01'},{'tags':['satisfaction-analog','complete-thing-in-one-pass'],'created_at':'2026-06-01'}]
r=measure_skew(rows)
assert r.fail_count==2 and r.analog_count==1, r
assert r.ratio_analog==2.0, r.ratio_analog
assert r.shape_distribution['complete-thing-in-one-pass']==1, r.shape_distribution
print('OK')
